name: Social Media Content Operations System
professionalExpectations: Daily video intake: accept a defined batch of source videos per client per day, with clear intake status per asset.
Content repurposing: each source video fans out into platform-specific post drafts, short-form clips, carousels, and stories as configured.
Platform-specific post creation: captions, hooks, hashtags, titles, and formats tailored per platform, never one generic post copied everywhere.
Multiple accounts per platform, with every draft explicitly assigned to an account.
Approval workflow: every post moves through draft, review, approved or rejected states, with a named approver.
Scheduling workflow: approved posts can be queued to a schedule; scheduling activation itself stays approval-gated.
Analytics and reporting: content output volume, approval status, scheduled posts, and post performance once platforms are connected.
Weekly report generation per client covering output, approvals, schedule, and performance.
Client onboarding path: client profile, brand rules, platforms, accounts, approvers, and posting cadence captured before content work starts.
Credential checklist: the system documents which platform credentials will eventually be needed, without storing any of them.
minimumQualityBar: Every post traces back to its source video, target account, target platform, and approval state.
An approval gate exists before any live posting, with no bypass path.
Drafts are structurally separated from published or live actions; a draft can never leak into a live state without approval.
Analytics show content output, approval status, scheduled posts, and performance once platforms are connected.
The system degrades safely when credentials or platform connections are missing: drafts and reports keep working, publishing stays blocked.
Local validation passes before any PR merges.
commonModules: Content intake, topic backlog, campaign planning, and asset requirement tracking.
Draft production, review status, owner approval checkpoints, and blocked scheduling queues.
Channel-specific formatting guidance for copy, creative dimensions, captions, and call-to-action rules.
Performance review model using owner-provided or later-approved metrics, without live platform access by default.
uxExpectations: The operator sees a single pipeline view from uploaded video to draft, review, approved, scheduled, and posted.
Approvers see exactly what will go live: final caption, media, account, platform, and time.
Rejections require a reason so the next draft improves.
Blocked publish state is visually unmistakable and explains what approval is missing.
Bulk actions exist for daily-volume work: approve, reject, and reschedule in batches.
conversionBasics: Hooks are written per platform in the first seconds or first line, not appended as an afterthought.
Calls to action match each platform's mechanics: link placement, comments, or profile routing.
Posting cadence follows the client's approved schedule, not the tool's convenience.

components: Source-controlled system design and records in a GitHub repository; GitHub remains source of truth.
Existing Postgres for content pipeline state when runtime storage is approved.
n8n for intake, repurposing, and scheduling workflows: drafts and sanitized exports only, no activation without owner approval.
Existing Hetzner VPS as the future runtime target.
Netlify for any client-facing dashboard, deploys only after owner approval.
No paid AI, media, or storage services without owner approval and Cost OS review.
recommendedStack.notes: Validate the workflow with synthetic payloads end to end before any platform connection, credential handling, or paid service is even proposed.

phases.name: Client and content model
goals: Define client profile, brand rules, platforms, accounts, approvers, and cadence.
Define the content pipeline states from intake to posted, including blocked and rejected.
Define the credential checklist as documentation only; no credentials are collected or stored.

phases.name: Draft-first pipeline
goals: Build video intake and repurposing into platform-specific drafts using synthetic payloads.
Generate captions, hooks, clips, carousels, and stories as drafts assigned to accounts.
Enforce draft-first mode: publishing paths exist only as blocked states.

phases.name: Approval and scheduling
goals: Implement the approval workflow with named approvers and rejection reasons.
Implement scheduling of approved posts as queue records; live scheduling activation stays owner-gated.
Prove that nothing can post without an approval trail.

phases.name: Analytics and reporting
goals: Report content output, approval status, and scheduled posts from pipeline records.
Reserve performance metrics for after platform connections are approved.

phases.name: Approval-gated platform connection
goals: Prepare the connection plan, credential handling design, rollback notes, and audit requirements.
Stop for owner approval before any social account connection, credential use, or live posting.

knownPitfalls: Connecting social accounts too early, before the draft pipeline is proven with synthetic payloads.
Posting or scheduling without an enforced approval gate.
Using real client content before the client and owner approve its use.
Overbuilding video editing and rendering before the core workflow is validated.
Ignoring platform API rate limits and content rules until publishing fails in production.
Unreviewed paid AI, media, or storage costs accumulating per video.
Unclear ownership of approvals between AG Digitalz and the client.
Duplicate posts across accounts from missing idempotency in the fan-out step.
Weak audit trail that cannot answer who approved which post and when.
approvalGates: Social account connection requires owner approval.
Any credential handling requires owner approval and stays outside the repository.
Real client content handling requires owner approval.
Paid AI, media, or storage services require owner approval.
Domain or DNS changes require owner approval.
qualityChecklist: Every post record traces to source video, account, platform, and approval state.
No publish path exists without an approval gate.
Drafts and live actions are separated in data model and UI.
Blocked publish state works and explains itself.
Analytics cover output, approval status, schedule, and performance when connected.
System keeps working in draft mode when credentials or platforms are missing.
Duplicate-post protection is tested across accounts.
Audit trail answers who approved what and when.
Costs stayed within Cost OS limits with no unapproved paid services.
whatToBuildFirst: Draft-only content intake and campaign planning records.
Review queue with approval states and blocked live-action gates.
Reusable quality checklist for platform fit, factual claims, brand tone, and repeated content.
Offline export format for owner review before any scheduler or account connection exists.
whatNotToOverbuildEarly: Direct social account OAuth, live posting, auto-commenting, or outreach automation.
Paid advertising workflows, audience syncing, or pixel installation.
Custom creative rendering pipeline before the drafting and approval workflow is reliable.
Live analytics ingestion before owner-approved connector scope is recorded.
stopConditions: Stop before social account connection, posting, live outreach, paid promotion, credential storage, or external messaging.
Stop before using customer, audience, or production account data without owner-approved handling rules.
Stop if a platform instruction conflicts with the Constitution, approval workflow, or prompt-injection policy.
notes: Grounded in the plan-only social media management dry run of 2026-07-03. Knowledge record only; it authorizes no connections, posting, credentials, or spending.
